name: GTandDiedTooSoons Max for Live Device Contracts
purpose: Source-only Max for Live device blueprints for the Live 12 downtempo punk-bluegrass production system.

notes: Commit reviewable Max patch source only.
Treat compiled Max for Live devices, Live sets, presets, samples, and renders as local or release artifacts.
Require a rollback copy and operator evidence before applying any device to a Live set.

devices.purpose: Translate AeroBand MIDI guitar/controller input into banjo-like picking articulation, velocity shaping, and pluck envelope controls.
implementation_hints: Preserve the performer input channel and avoid destructive quantization.
Expose velocity and pluck controls as Live parameters for clip-envelope automation.
Keep the output MIDI-only until the downstream banjo instrument rack is selected locally.

devices.purpose: Vary banjo roll density and timing within bounded musical ranges while preserving bluegrass forward-roll identity.
implementation_hints: Use deterministic seeds in test mode and bounded randomness in performance mode.
Never emit unbounded note storms.
Keep probability changes auditable as macro automation.

devices.purpose: Route fiddle, guitar, and hybrid string phrases into controlled call-and-response gestures across sections.
implementation_hints: Keep routing deterministic by scene and clip region.
Expose response probability and send depth as Live automation targets.
Avoid routing private or unapproved source audio.

devices.purpose: Constrain mandolin and boom-chuck guitar chops to the intended rhythmic grid while retaining human offbeat push.
implementation_hints: Guard against accidental double triggers.
Expose swing and ghost-note probability as safe bounded controls.
Use Live tempo and scene position rather than a private external clock.

devices.purpose: Shape dobro and metallic slide gestures into spectral repeats without embedding source audio in the device.
implementation_hints: Reference only the host track signal.
Keep feedback bounded to prevent runaway gain.
Do not bundle samples, presets, or commercial content.

devices.purpose: Keep upright bass and sub movement anchored to the track key center while allowing house-style sidechain and note-length control.
implementation_hints: Constrain generated pitch output to approved scale tones.
Expose sub blend and note length as automation-safe macros.
Avoid writing audio or sidechain renders to the repository.

devices.purpose: Trigger punk drum fills at section boundaries with bounded probability and parallel-crush coordination.
implementation_hints: Prefer scene and bar-position conditions over free-running randomness.
Cap fill density to protect arrangement clarity.
Keep the output MIDI-only until Live drum devices are selected locally.

devices.purpose: Coordinate deep-house machine accents, swing, and filter motion against the bluegrass pulse.
implementation_hints: Use Live transport timing as the authority.
Expose accent and filter controls as automation-safe parameters.
Keep generated MIDI bounded to the selected scene length.

devices.purpose: Gate and document public-domain source deck usage without storing raw source audio or rights-uncertain material in the patch.
implementation_hints: Require rights metadata before enabling source playback.
Keep source file references local and redacted from Git.
Expose a visible provenance hold control for operator review.

devices.purpose: Create alien spatial bloom from project audio using bounded width, azimuth, freeze, and grain-density controls.
implementation_hints: Keep freeze buffers local to Live runtime.
Bound width and azimuth changes to avoid unsafe monitoring surprises.
Avoid committing rendered spatial audio or device binaries.
